user_message: Working on Litware Inc's account health. Got the escalation email -- help me identify the blockers.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-02-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:07:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:12:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:17:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:22:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:26:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:28:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:31:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: solution sketch needed from discovery signals

required_artifacts: BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Discovery Call Transcript -> Opportunity Build template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
